text.title: I Exposed China’s Most Extreme Street Food!!
text.u: Look how full that walk is.
>> Welcome to the Silk Road and the most
most definitely heard of this route from
Shien to Rome, but did you know this
route has some of the wildest foods on
>> Oh my god, you're not going to be ready
>> And in this ultimate Muslim Chinese
>> as soon as the lid came off, it just hit
>> We're taking you on a 7-day non-stop
mission to expose the region's most
like you've never seen before. This is
>> And by the end, we'll decide whether
this region is secretly China's most
Let's just get right into it. But first
up, before it gets super crazy, I'm
taking my friend Alex, who's just landed
in China for his first time ever, and
taking him in straight deep into the
local market. It gets really good, guys.
Just watch. So, in today's video, you
guys are going to want to watch the
whole thing because we got a good
variety of Muslim Chinese and non-Muslim
>> And to start out, we're going to go to a
morning market. Okay, first half is
going to be non-Muslim Chinese. Second
half of the video is going to be full-on
halal Chinese street food. It's going to
>> Okay, and first up, we're going to go
visit a morning market. This is a super
busy and get some local breakfast. And
here we have a special tunga. This is a
special date cake. It's a sweet
>> Money's almost out. It looks like
>> Oh, Jinga. Oh, yeah. Yeah. The the
>> za. Oh, da. Okay. So, it's got date and
>> Yeah. So, you can see it's a sticky rice
and then there's layers. There's dates
and beans and it's sweet and uh it's
>> It's like a nice morning uh sweet
>> We're going to get a small portion.
>> It looks so thick, doesn't it?
>> So, it's 5. It's about.7 cents for a
portion. I say what? It's really
>> I love how everyone's taking a video of
this place. Must be really popular.
>> It's like a thick sweet date bean
>> It's so warm and sticky. It's like
>> It's sweet, but it's not like overly
sweet. I thought it was going to be like
really sickly, but it's not. And then
the beans adding a bit of savory.
>> he says it's good in the afternoon once
>> Yeah. Tastes pretty good to me.
>> I don't think it's going to survive till
a breakfast jenb crap. We got to go
Oh, this is Jenbang. Take a look at
>> This is the morning breakfast. Chinese
>> So, what is it? It's like a thick dough.
>> It's like a wheat. Yeah, it's a wheat
Okay. So, we're going to get a classic
>> It's so thick. Breakfast crepe. Chinese
>> We're going to get two eggs. Oh, look at
that. So, the eggs go on.
I love the little girl in the background
just so you know ice [laughter] cream.
>> This is going to be good.
>> This is why you have to come with
Trevor. I wouldn't stand 5 minutes in
>> Oh, it's their signature sauce. So, we
got some chili and then some signature
>> Again, spread with an industrial size
paintbrush. That is always a good sign.
>> Exactly. Oh, it's going to scrape it
Jatai. That's pickled veg going in. Some
>> See the coriander and a bit of lettuce.
>> Oh, this is a crispy wafer.
Crispy wafer. And then wrap it up.
>> It's such simple ingredients. I wasn't
expecting much. Then you have a malf.
>> Yeah. It's absolutely packed, but it's
full of like local people just trying to
get their vegetables for cooking later
in the day. It feels like rustic and
authentic. It's not like touristy at
>> Exactly. Okay, so we're going to enter
into a pan fried dumpling heaven up
here. Just found it. Looks really good.
>> I can see them. Let's go try it out. Up
>> Look at this. They just added them in.
>> Oh, so we got two two fillings. Pork and

chives. He's just going to add water in.
>> Oh, here comes the flip. Look at this.
Look at the yellow sizzle on the bottom.
>> This is what you were looking for.
>> This is exactly what I was coming to
>> I was like, I want dumplings.
>> They look so good, don't they?
So, we're going to get two of this
>> So, we're going to get two of this
pepper soup. your dofu, your muiranga,
>> and the five spice chili, the five spice
>> That's the biggest wooden spoon I've
ever seen in my life, by the way.
>> And the secret is comes with a little
You just You can just pour a little.
>> Yeah. Yeah. Chinese vinegar is so famous
and so delicious. Like it's it elevates
>> The vinegar definitely cuts through like
the fat, the oil of it. Have I got
I was like, where's the pork?
>> So, it's one ramen beef for three
dumplings. So, it's about 15 cents for
Handmade handkerchief. It cannot get any
>> Okay. And next up, it's going to be a
crazy day. We're going to the origin of
>> This is the birthplace of the hamburger.
>> Oh my god. Now you're talking my
>> It's a theory. It's a theory. I think
it's true. And here, let's go get in the
let's do this. Time for the burger.
>> Origins of the burger. Let's go.
>> Here we are. Rojamu. A classic Chian
street food. Number one in my opinion.
It's a beautiful pork pa. Yeah. Fully
Everyone's just eating right on the
>> Always good when it's this busy straight
>> Line up and get one. Going to get a big
>> So, this is this is jelly noodle with
chili oil. Oh, look at the color on
>> Here's the pork. Look at that.
>> We came to the right place. We came to
>> Okay. And this is where it starts. I was

>> So, you going to slice it open

>> Look at that. He puts a whole slice of
>> A whole slice of the skin.
>> When he literally just gets a whole
slice of the skin with the fat and
>> Okay. [laughter] And then we got the
>> I've got the noodles. Look at this.
>> They fully loaded them. They fully
>> Look at those. The origins of burgers.
I've never seen something like it.
>> They loaded it with the skin.
>> I wasn't expecting that. I thought he
was going to throw it away and he just
keeps the whole skin just throw the
>> Oh, and that bread as well is really
>> Wow. The jiggle. Look at the jiggle.
>> And they stew that pork in a light soy
and a five spice. Oh, is that what it
>> And it's so soft. It's going to Oh, man.
I don't think we can hold it.
>> They invented the burger. I'm giving.
>> They invented the burger. Look at that.
>> The skin is salty. It's fatty.
>> I love the bread. The bread's really
crispy, but then the filling is like
>> Oh my god. It's so packed with flavor,
>> And it's got that It's seasoned nicely.
That pie spice soy. The light soy, the
>> If you're watching this, Tian, you have
What are these noodles? Talk to me.
>> Now we got the lang. These are the jelly
rice jelly noodle loaded with their

they put sesame in there. They put a
little light soy, celery, and you just
mix it up. Man, I'm really proud. I can
take you to taste all these [laughter]
cuz for me, the first time I tasted
these, it was like a life-changing
>> Like you come to China, you taste this
>> That is drenched in chili oil. It's just
like a river of chili oil underneath.
You definitely got to mix it around.
>> As soon as you put in the sesame and
then the chili comes in afterwards.
>> Thank you. Thank you. Thank you very
>> Okay. Next up, we are in a Muslim
district. Now, this is old Shien, the
ancient quarter. It's full of history
and there's so much delicious food here
and we're going to see what we can find.
>> It's going to be amazing. Okay. And if I
remember correctly, we're going to go
down this deep alleyway here and there's
some hidden noodle joint. Like really
>> You'd never think to come down here.
>> Like 10 years ago, we came exploring and
just walking down the alleyways and
>> this little jelly noodle joint and it
turned out to be incredible. And it's
like this run by like a local lady
that's been making it her whole life.
>> And it's just, if I remember correct,
[laughter] this is the alleyway. And it
should just Oh, yeah. It's just right up
here. It's right up here. Take a look.
>> Oh, this is the spot. This is the spot.
Oh, look at this. This is This is rice
>> So, she's slicing up the rice jelly.
>> What is this? It's like a block of what?
>> And then look at this. This is the
>> That's like the peppery That's the
>> The consistency is thick, isn't it?
So, she loads up and then let's go see
The most familiar place to see what she
>> Is she going to do it for us or we
>> No, we got to do it. [laughter]
So, we got to break these.
>> Okay. So, we're breaking up this pa.
>> We're a bit of We're experts at this
>> You got to break up the mo. This is
>> You know what this means? Loads of
goodness. Get soaked up. Real good.
here we are. I just had this image of
dropping this bowl. I was like having to
So, we're going to get the sentry egg
>> Oh, look at this. Here's the sentry egg.
>> What do they call it? Century egg.
>> 100-y old egg. Well, it's not actually,
but they ferment it for quite a while.
>> It's a sulfurous egg. You look at the
>> Oh my god. is black inside as well.
>> It's black inside. They ferment it.
>> This is going to be a strong flavor.
>> Strong. Have you ever had century eggs?
>> I don't know. I don't think so.
>> It's like a rice starch jelly.
>> This is for me one of the top dishes of
Shien. Look at that. She's loading it
>> a nice uniform, isn't it? The way that
>> And there's a lot of ingredients that go
into this. It's it's it's not simple
>> Yeah. So, this is just the first layer.
There's more coming. This is the lugger.
gravy. It's like a peppery gravy. Wow.
>> It's a thick It's so thick.

>> Wow. Look at the color on that. This is
>> This is the signature. This is jermaang.
And theno chili oil. Look at that.
>> The color of that chili oil. And then
>> Okay. So, she says don't mix it. Eat it
from the side. That's a masterpiece.
This is really the the the hay culture
here. You can see there's so many
different flavors in there. The sesame
paste, the chili oil. Let's just go in.
>> Look at the color on that.
>> I'm excited for you to try your first

Yeah. This is This is full flavor. This
is one of my favorite flavor combos in

just want to eat it from the side. So,
you just take a scoop from the side. Oh,
look at that. Look at that.

>> I'm not going to lie to you people on

>> Yang. Yeah. Lamb fat and beef.
>> Oh, that's the secret. It's got cumin.
>> That's why you love it so much. Now I've
He's done it for 10 years here.
>> It's just It's just beef and onion.
>> He's not disclosing his secret recipe,
That is so cool to see.
>> It's like a central Asian like
>> Yeah. You see them in like Kyrgyzstan
and Kazakhstan, all that kind of region.
Then you see them in the middle of Shian
in China. It's the silk road. It's the
>> And you see they're just starting to
turn a golden brown on the bottom. The
first ones that you put in.
>> I wonder how he knows when they're done.
>> Maybe when they start falling off.
>> yeah. It's risky. You don't want them to
>> Exactly. It's literal money down the
>> Moment of truth. There we go.
